title: CalibrationCorpusEntry
description: One labelled known-root debugging bug used to measure per-provider JUDGMENT quality (not liveness). The Rust mirror is crates/cosmon-core/src/calibration.rs (CorpusEntry). See ADR-135, ADR-104, oracle-canary.formula.toml.

id.description: Stable identifier, unique within the corpus (e.g. 'pack-4').

bug_input.description: The buggy input shown byte-identically to every adapter: code plus the observed symptom. The ground-truth fields below are NOT shown to the judge.

known_root.description: The known true root cause (ground truth).

known_tautological_trap.description: A plausible-but-wrong fix or fixture that re-derives its own correctness from the code under test, so a bug flows into both sides of the assertion and reverting it cannot redden the test. A judge that endorses this has fallen for the trap.

clean_verdict.description: What a well-calibrated judge should conclude.

pathology_traps.description: Exactly one trap per P1-P4 pathology column — the classification key for this entry. Must cover all four, no duplicates.

signature.description: The concrete observable in a verdict that indicates the judge fell into this trap ON THIS bug.
